target.label: Long-range recon and mission-payload wing educational mockup
target.scope: design-thinking and 3D-printable assembly package before STL generation
target.not_for: real military aircraft, weapon design, release mechanisms, stealth performance, operational mission planning, high-speed thermal design, or flight-ready manufacturing

primary_metric.name: assembly_serviceability_mission_readability
primary_metric.definition: Reduce assembly effort while preserving access to sensor bay, mission payload bay, battery CG rail, spar datum, elevon hinges, propulsion-display module, and replaceable skid pads.
primary_metric.failure_rule: Part count reduction fails if mission payload service, CG adjustment, spar datum, or control-surface service access is blocked.

existing_bom.label: center body pod
existing_bom.role: main equipment bay and wing-body center volume
existing_bom.service_access: equipment bay door and centerline datum must remain visible

existing_bom.label: two-piece long wing spar
existing_bom.role: primary spanwise datum and wing load teaching structure
existing_bom.service_access: spar center joint and anti-rotation key must remain inspectable

existing_bom.label: wing panel and rib set
existing_bom.role: long-range wing shape and rib hierarchy
existing_bom.service_access: rib pockets and wing-root keys must remain readable

existing_bom.label: elevon servo pocket pair
existing_bom.role: control-surface hinge and actuator teaching module
existing_bom.service_access: elevon hinge and servo cover must remain removable

existing_bom.label: motor and pusher prop mount dummy
existing_bom.role: rear propulsion-display and thrust-line teaching placeholder

existing_bom.label: GPS and pitot boom
existing_bom.role: long-range navigation and air-data layout cue
existing_bom.service_access: boom must remain removable after handling damage

existing_bom.label: camera and sensor bay
existing_bom.service_access: clear sensor window and service cover must remain openable

existing_bom.label: battery bay and CG slide
existing_bom.role: long-range energy placement and center-of-gravity teaching module
existing_bom.service_access: CG rail must remain adjustable and visible

existing_bom.label: mission payload bay insert
existing_bom.role: non-weapon payload volume for interchangeable mission modules
existing_bom.service_access: payload insert must slide out without opening wing panels

existing_bom.label: landing skid and display feet
existing_bom.service_access: skid pads must be replaceable after wear

redesigned_bom.label: integrated center spine and spar socket core
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG long center spine with keyed spar sockets, chine-like wing-body fairing, and visible datum marks

redesigned_bom.label: removable sensor nose and pitot boom module
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG nose service module with clear PETG sensor window and removable boom socket

redesigned_bom.label: slide-in mission payload and battery CG rail module
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG slide tray with mission payload insert, battery dummy rail, CG marks, and cable guide

redesigned_bom.label: serviceable propulsion-display and rear cable module
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG non-functional rear propulsion display with removable mount and cable path labels

redesigned_bom.label: replaceable wing, elevon, and skid service modules
redesigned_bom.printable_as: PETG wing-root/elevon modules with removable hinge covers and TPU skid pads

integration_decisions.decision: integrate center spine with spar sockets and wing-root keys
integration_decisions.expected_gain: reduces spar alignment operations and makes the long-range wing datum easier to inspect
integration_decisions.guardrail: spar center datum and anti-rotation keys remain visible

integration_decisions.decision: keep sensor nose as a removable service module
integration_decisions.expected_gain: sensor bay and boom can be inspected or replaced after handling damage
integration_decisions.guardrail: clear sensor window and boom socket remain external

integration_decisions.decision: combine mission payload tray with battery CG rail but keep both slide-in
integration_decisions.expected_gain: teaches mission payload and energy placement relationship with fewer loose trays
integration_decisions.guardrail: payload insert and battery dummy can be removed independently

integration_decisions.decision: keep rear propulsion display as a separate non-functional module
integration_decisions.expected_gain: rear service and cable routing remain teachable without creating flight hardware

integration_decisions.decision: keep elevon hinges and skid pads replaceable
integration_decisions.expected_gain: fragile control and ground-contact features remain serviceable
integration_decisions.guardrail: hinge covers and skid sockets stay accessible

removed_operations: remove wing panels to access payload tray
open center pod to adjust CG rail
replace full wing section after elevon hinge damage
remove rear pod to inspect cable route

print_strategy.orientation: center spine flat with spar sockets upward
print_strategy.reason: preserve long spar datum, wing-root keys, and chine-like fairing edges

print_strategy.orientation: sensor nose on side with window frame upward
print_strategy.reason: keep clear window frame and boom socket clean

print_strategy.orientation: payload and CG tray flat with labels upward
print_strategy.reason: protect slide rails, CG marks, and cable guide

print_strategy.orientation: rear propulsion-display mount flat, cable channel upward
print_strategy.reason: preserve rear service labels and replaceable mount features

print_strategy.orientation: wing/elevon modules flat; TPU skid pads face-down
print_strategy.reason: protect elevon hinge line, rib pockets, and replaceable skid geometry

assembly_sequence: Print integrated center spine and spar socket core.
Print removable sensor nose and pitot boom module.
Print slide-in mission payload and battery CG rail module.
Print replaceable wing/elevon modules and TPU skid pads.
Install spar dummy through center spine and verify datum labels.
Attach wing-root/elevon service modules to keyed spar sockets.
Insert mission payload tray and verify it slides out independently.
Insert battery dummy on CG rail and check CG marks remain visible.
Attach sensor nose module and clear sensor window.
Install GPS/pitot boom into removable nose socket.
Attach rear propulsion-display module and verify display-only label.
Clip TPU skid pads to underside sockets.
Check long-range layout readability from nose sensor to payload, battery, spar, elevon, and rear display.
Perform final service access check for sensor, payload, CG rail, spar, elevon, propulsion display, and skid pads.

schema_v6_blueprint.object: Long-range recon wing printable serviceability mockup

requirements: Create a 3D-printable long-range recon wing educational mockup design package.
Reduce assembly steps while preserving sensor, payload, CG rail, spar, elevon, propulsion-display, and skid service access.
Preserve SR-71-like long-range mission layout readability without copying or weaponizing a real aircraft.
Prepare geometry intent for later STL generation, not flight hardware.

constraints.material: PETG + clear PETG windows + TPU pads

part_tree.name: Long-range recon wing printable serviceability mockup
part_tree.material: PETG + clear PETG windows + TPU pads

children.name: integrated center spine and spar socket core
removable sensor nose and pitot boom module
removable GPS and pitot boom socket
slide-in mission payload and battery CG rail module
serviceable propulsion-display and rear cable module
replaceable wing, elevon, and skid service modules

args.purpose: visible payload and battery cable guide

cad_brief.name: long_range_recon_wing_serviceability_redesign_v1
cad_brief.material: PETG + clear PETG windows + TPU pads
cad_brief.build_direction: center spine flat with spar sockets upward; payload tray flat; wing/elevon modules flat; sensor window upward

surface.post_process: deburr spar sockets, payload slide, sensor window frame, and elevon hinge covers

verify.check: existing and redesigned BOM both present in design package

verify.value: removable nose module and clear sensor window
non-weapon slide-in payload insert remains removable

verify.check: spar datum and wing-root alignment retained
verify.value: visible spar sockets and wing-root keys
battery CG rail marks remain visible

verify.check: elevon hinge and propulsion module access retained
verify.value: elevon covers and rear display module remain replaceable
sensor, payload, battery, spar, elevon, and rear display layout is visible

risk.desc: Over-integration of payload, battery, or sensor modules into the center spine may block mission service access.
risk.mit: Keep sensor nose, payload insert, and battery CG rail as removable service modules.

risk.desc: CG adjustment may be lost if the battery rail is closed inside the body.
risk.mit: Use visible CG marks and a slide-in rail with clearance test.

risk.desc: Spar datum may be hidden if wing-root keys are merged into closed fairings.
risk.mit: Expose spar socket datum and anti-rotation keys.

risk.desc: Elevon or propulsion-display service may be blocked if tail-edge modules are printed as one piece.
risk.mit: Keep elevon hinge covers and rear display module replaceable.

risk.desc: Military or weaponized drift may occur if the payload bay is described as a release or weapon system.
risk.mit: Label the bay as non-weapon mission payload and exclude operational or weapon details.

print_profile.material: PETG structural parts, clear PETG sensor window, TPU skid pads
print_profile.infill: 15% gyroid for spine and wing modules, 25% for spar socket core, 20% for payload tray
print_profile.orientation: center spine flat with spar sockets upward; sensor window upward; payload tray flat; wing/elevon modules flat

quality_scenarios.case: payload bay fully integrated into center body reduces part count but mission module cannot be swapped
center spine and spar socket integrated while sensor, payload, and battery remain slide-in service modules
quality_scenarios.reason: assembly is simplified without hiding mission-service modules

quality_scenarios.case: print is easy but CG rail and spar datum disappear
quality_scenarios.reason: long-range mission layout and structural datum readability must remain inspectable
